signature_questions.what_they_press_on: How itinerary length and shorter itineraries (on-ramps) factor into new-to-cruise recovery and whether changes are structural, plus any incremental costs of a higher mix of short itineraries (asked in China redeployment context and in Q2 booking/itinerary question).

topics.what_they_press_on: How itinerary length and shorter itineraries (on-ramps) factor into new-to-cruise recovery and whether changes are structural, plus any incremental costs of a higher mix of short itineraries (asked in China redeployment context and in Q2 booking/itinerary question).
Seeks color on shoreside labor cost trends and whether wage pressure is easing as firms tap international labor; frames as a modeling input for cost structure (2022-Q1).

distinctive_concerns.blind_spots: Fewer than 5 topics meet the ≥2-turn threshold; n_topics meeting threshold = 3. Notably, he has 0 turns on fuel hedging/consumption details despite peers asking about fuel and hedging in 2022-Q2 (Vince Ciepiel asked about fuel mix and hedging). He also rarely asks for explicit per-ALBD consumption metrics, detailed yield decomposition (fare vs onboard), or hedging/FX sensitivity.
distinctive_concerns.stance_drift: Early 2022 focus was on market reentry and labor (2022-Q1: China and shoreside labor). By 2022-Q2–2023-Q1 his emphasis shifted toward booking/inventory strategy and wave management (2022-Q2 & 2023-Q1) and he added questions about marketing economics and capital/ESG tradeoffs by 2022-Q3 (Trifecta/ROIC question).

coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Title appears as Senior US Payments / Lifestyle / Digital Commerce Analyst (Macquarie). He is routinely called by name and given multiple turns late in Q&A (e.g. #9–#11), suggesting an established, mid-senior sell-side role rather than a junior repeat-questioner. Management treats him as a regular, specialty analyst across calls (2022-Q1 through 2023-Q1).
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Frames Royal Caribbean through a consumer payments / digital commerce and marketing lens within cruise/leisure — focused on customer acquisition economics, booking behavior, and deployment strategy rather than deep technical shipping operations. Questions bridge travel/leisure demand topics and payments/marketing unit economics (2022-Q3, 2023-Q1).
rhetorical_signature: Polite openers/closers: opens with "Thanks so much" in 5 of 11 turns (e.g. 2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q1).
Frequently signals a second question: explicitly says "then I have a follow-up" (2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3).
Tone: concise, non-confrontational; asks 1–2 focused parts and often thanks before/after management answers.

reasoning_style.justification: He asks operational and strategic questions that are model-relevant (booking curve, inventory/wave, marketing cost per customer) while also seeking qualitative context (China reentry, sustainability prioritization) — e.g. booking/inventory questions (2022-Q2, 2023-Q1) and marketing unit-cost (2022-Q3).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Mostly multi-part: he explicitly appends follow-ups in 4 of 11 turns (≈36%) and frequently takes a second turn on the same call. He rarely pushes aggressively; interactions are clarifying rather than combative.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He asks for explicit guidance and unit economics ("what expectations you have, in terms of a normalized period" (2022-Q1); "any costs associated" (2022-Q2); "cheaper or easier to acquire customers per unit" (2022-Q3)). n_turns using the term 'bps' = 0; he prefers explicit timeframes, costs, and mechanisms over abstract commentary.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He anchors to management's most-recent lines or prepared remarks (e.g. "in the prepared remarks, you commented" 2022-Q1) and to company programs (references to "WAVE" in 2022-Q3 and wave cycle in 2023-Q1). There is little evidence he anchors to peers systematically.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #8–#10 of the queue; across 11 turns he was #10 on 5 of 11 calls, #8 twice, #9 twice, #7 once, and #11 once.
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When earlier in the rotation (e.g. #7 on 2022-Q2; #9 on 2022-Q1) he opens on demand/deployment topics — China/itinerary redeployment (2022-Q1) or shorter itineraries/new-to-cruise (2022-Q2).

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Evidence: when booking/itinerary themes are on the table he asks a deeper curve/structural question (2022-Q2: "closer-in trend abate"), quoting "closer-in trend abate".
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.cross_narrative_reconcile: Evidence: after management discussed Trifecta and carbon reduction, he asked to reconcile ROIC/earnings versus carbon efforts (2022-Q3), quoting "ROIC and earnings relative to deleveraging".
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reactive_followup: Evidence: frequently signals and takes a follow-up turn to drill deeper into the same topic (2022-Q1: asked China then "I have a follow-up on labor"), quoting "then I have a follow-up".

white_space_topics.label: Trifecta priority: ROIC vs carbon reduction (capital allocation tradeoffs)

cross_analyst_reactivity.explicit_peer_references: n_turns = 0; no evidence in history of him explicitly naming or following another analyst's question (no phrases like "following up on X's question" found).
cross_analyst_reactivity.management_line_anchoring: Count = 2; he explicitly anchors to management's prepared remarks or program lines (e.g. "in the prepared remarks, you commented" 2022-Q1; references company program "WAVE" in 2022-Q3).
cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He routinely takes a second turn on the same call to clarify or drill deeper: in all 4 distinct calls covered (2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q1) he used multiple turns (took a follow-up) to get more detail, generally for clarification or deeper drilling rather than to introduce a new unrelated topic.

framing_overrides_by_theme.booking_dynamics: Tracks whether close-in booking pattern is post-COVID transient or structural, and how it reshapes wave management.
framing_overrides_by_theme.capital_allocation_pattern: Orders the Trifecta initiatives (ROIC, deleverage, decarbonization) by priority.

_built_from: v7: company-layer split + probe/topic structure + v6 prose
